task: Should the benchmark dashboard prioritize live status visibility or dense historical analytics on the first screen?
Is a mechanism switch banner more useful when it is prominent or when it is understated?
Should a replayable benchmark report favor brevity or complete traceability?
When a benchmark is still running, should the UI optimize for progress feedback or detailed telemetry?
Is it better for a multi-agent system to expose disagreement early or wait until consensus is reached?
Should a product demo emphasize raw performance numbers or the reasoning behind the result?
For a technical operator, is trust built faster through receipts or through concise summaries?
Should the default benchmark view foreground accuracy or cost efficiency?
When the system lacks full telemetry, should it show approximations or explicitly mark missing data?
Is a debate-driven decision process more persuasive when it shows intermediate disagreement?
Should the benchmark wizard default to templates or custom questions?
For onboarding new users, should the UI start with a curated example or a blank slate?
Is a compact summary more effective than a detailed report for first-time benchmark viewers?
Should a benchmark run page focus on the active stream or the final artifact?
Is it better to optimize the interface for one successful path or for broad exploratory use?
When results vary by model, should the dashboard highlight the best model or the full spread?
Should a live benchmark report privilege certainty or nuance?
Is a visible cost estimate more persuasive than an accuracy estimate for engineering stakeholders?
Should the demo category use realistic product language or explicit benchmark jargon?
When a system is partially offline, should the UI stay available with degraded data or block access entirely?
